Ad Text:
About us:

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation.  As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world’s most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here. 

About the role:

Working from Mawson Lakes, the Senior Cost & Schedule Analyst will provide subject matter expertise to Program Managers and Control Account Managers. 

Your day to day responsibilities will include:

Manage the establishment and maintenance of fully integrated schedule and cost baselines for the program

Provide SME guidance to stakeholders in the development of appropriate Work Breakdown Structures (WBS) for upcoming Tasking Statements, and manage the development and maintenance of existing Contract WBS and associate Dictionaries

Manage the day-to-day tasking of Project Controls team members within your project(s)

Oversee the development and implementation of the Earned Value Management System in conjunction with PCG functional staff to facilitate the successful achievement of project objectives

Incorporate risk and schedule margin tasks into schedules based on risk registers and undertake Schedule Risk Analysis as required

Ensure all project documentation including the Work Authorisation Documents (WADs, Control Account Plans and Responsibility Assignment Matrix are updated and maintained in accordance with the CSI Earned Value Management Plan

About you:

Experience in tasking small to medium teams

Expert level experience in applying principles, concepts, practices and standards of scheduling and EVM principles, in particular, Critical Path Method and Baseline Control preferably within the defence industry

Extensive project administration experience with knowledge of and exposure to accounting and budgeting principles

Computer literacy with an ability to prepare, analyse, and interpret reports

Intermediate to advanced skills and experience in the use of MS Office and other relevant software packages and applications (Open Plan Professional, Cobra, etc.)

Extensive experience within a customer liaison role

Excellent communication skills including the ability to prepare and present concise oral and written reports, briefings, updates and other documentation to internal stakeholders

Some bid proposal development experience will be an added advantage

How we reward and support you:

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.
We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

Flexible work & Hybrid work options

Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

Additional benefits such as Annual Flu Vaccinations, Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs

Special Requirements:

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

Equal Opportunity Statement:

Lockheed Martin Australia supports Equal Employment Opportunity and values diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, free of unlawful discrimination and is harassment free, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. By leveraging our employees’ unique talents and experiences, we will deliver innovative, affordable solutions and unparalleled customer value.

As part of our endeavours to increase participation of Indigenous Australians, we welcome applications from all people, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are selected for this role, we would be happy to discuss any accommodations that will help you to be successful.

Company description:



About VIDA

About VIDA The Victorian Infrastructure Delivery Authority (VIDA) is a specialist project delivery organisation, responsible for delivering the biggest transport and health infrastructure program in Victorian history. This includes more than 200 projects such as the Metro Tunnel, North East Link, West Gate Tunnel, removing level crossings, new and upgraded hospitals in Footscray, Frankston and Warrnambool, and specialist programs.



Job description:



About VIDA Health

VIDA Health (Victorian Health Building Authority) is responsible for the rollout of the Victorian Government's multi-billion-dollar health capital projects. We're delivering world-class health facilities across Victoria including new public hospitals and emergency care, community health services, ambulance branches, residential aged care and mental health facilities. VIDA Health also provides technical support across engineering, property, asset management and environmental sustainability disciplines.

The Opportunity

As Manager, Project Data Governance, you will lead a team of analysts within the Project Controls branch to ensure project data is accurate, consistent, well-governed and fit for purpose. Your work will underpin critical internal and external reporting to senior executives, Ministers, central agencies and the public.

This is a senior leadership role focused on establishing strong data governance frameworks, driving quality and assurance across project reporting, and enabling meaningful insights through modern analytics and visualisation tools.

Working closely with project teams, Reporting & Analytics, and key stakeholders across VIDA and government, you will ensure project data supports informed decision-making across the full project lifecycle.


Key aspects of the role include:

Establishing and maintaining data governance standards, definitions and quality frameworks for project data.
Leading a team of analysts to deliver high-quality insights and dashboards using tools such as Power BI.
Overseeing the coordination, quality assurance and delivery of monthly project and program reporting.
Supporting external reporting, ministerial briefs and audit requirements with defensible, accurate data.
Driving innovation and continuous improvement across project data, reporting and governance practices.
Ensuring compliance with legislative, privacy and information security requirements.

We are looking for:

A data governance or reporting leader with experience in complex, enterprise-scale environments.
Strong expertise in data quality, governance frameworks and assurance processes
Experience working with analytics and reporting platforms such as Power BI, Excel/Power Query, SharePoint and SQL-based environments.
Proven ability to translate complex data into clear insights for executive and non-technical audiences.
A confident leader who builds capability, influences stakeholders and drives continuous improvement.

To learn more about the opportunity and view the full requirements please download a copy of the Position Description HERE.

Qualifications
Tertiary qualification in data governance, business, analytics, risk, project management or a related discipline, or demonstrated equivalent professional experience.
Experience within project controls, PMO or large infrastructure delivery environments will be highly regarded.

Interested in working with VIDA Health?

Please click the "Apply" button to submit a copy of your resume and a cover letter expressing your interest and suitability for the role.

We don't want you to miss out! Please only apply for this position via the "Apply" button as emailed applications will not be considered.

Applications close 11.59pm on Wednesday 1st of April 2026.

If you require any adjustment to the recruitment and selection process or need an alternative format to any of the application material, please contact TalentPeopleandCapability.vhba@vida.vic.gov.au.

Further information

Want to know more about VIDA Health? Check out our website and LinkedIn.

VIDA Health is an equal opportunity employer, committed to fostering a values-led, diverse and inclusive workplace. We believe our teams should represent the diversity of our communities in which we work, and we actively embrace and celebrate people from all walks of life.

All prospective employees undergo a National Criminal Records Check. You must be an Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident or hold a valid work permit or visa. Preferred candidates will be subject to pre-employment checks including, but not limited to, police checks, reference checks, qualification checks and role related clearances.

Hello. We’re Virgin Australia. We’re the airline that’s always done things a little different. Our way. The Virgin way. We’re on a mission to make flying better than ‘nice’. And to do that, it takes a team of talented people who are passionate about customer experience, doing the right thing always, with a focus on safety – in the air, on the ground and in our workplaces. A team who values real diversity and inclusion, in a big brand with big ambitions.

What you’ll be doing:

The Digital Business Analyst role is to assist our Product Owners in bringing our Digital strategy to life by building features, products and experiences that our guests love. Sitting within Digital Squads, the Digital Business Analyst develops and improves processes to optimise Virgin Australia’s digital presence and performance. Play a pivotal role in gathering and elaborating business and technical requirements to enable OOSD convergence and delivery of the new VABF and next-generation retailing capabilities, while triaging core issues to assess severity and business impact, ensuring alignment to DX parity, commercial priorities, and the broader transformation roadmap.

You’ll be great in this role if you have:

5+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst
Experience in owning the stakeholder engagement and requirements gathering phase of a project
Experience and ability to define the requirements and deliverables that should be completed as part of a project
Experience and confidence in liaising with stakeholders at all levels
Proven experience in analysing and documenting user requirements and business processes
Proven experience on large and complex digital projects
Experience liaising with digital development and test teams
Strong understanding of Agile methodologies
Hands on experience with Sabre or similar airline systems and APIs. 

Desirable skills:

Industry standard Business Analysis certification
Travel industry experience, understanding of core airline - systems
Understanding of IT security protocols
Basic understanding of the Australian Privacy Principals
Experience working in an environment with a high workload and competing priorities
Experience in working in squad models
Understanding of Offer and Order or NDC

We’re committed to looking after you, with some of the best benefits and conditions in the industry – including (but not limited to):

Discounted flights for you and your loved ones (including $1000 worth of travel credits per year)
A flexible, hybrid work style. Work from home or the office, adapt your location to suit your style and needs
One of Brisbane’s most admired head office locations, with South Bank train station, a supermarket, pharmacy, and lunch options downstairs plus a 5-minute walk to the Busway, a rooftop restaurant and onsite gym (with a low membership fee) and South Bank Parklands just across the road
Discounts on travel insurance, lounge membership, car hire, accommodation, and experiences worldwide
Cheaper hospitality, retail, technology, beauty and wellness services
Free counselling and access to a wellbeing app to support your physical, mental, social and financial health
‘Dress for Your Day’ – enjoy the freedom to wear whatever makes you feel great for the type of work you do and the day you have ahead of you.

When everyone is included, everyone wins:

We’re all individuals. And we love that. That’s why we encourage applications from Indigenous Australians, people with disability, those from diverse cultural backgrounds, our friends in the LGBTQI+ community, and anyone else who wants to join our family.

Ready to apply? We’re ready to hear from you. Apply now.

This is a Permanent Full-Time position based in Sydney/Melbourne/Adelaide/Brisbane/Perth.

AEMO at the Heart of Energy

 AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this.

We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection.

 Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector!

 About the Team & the Position

 The System Design Division plays a critical role in AEMO’s success, driving the organisation’s collaborative and industry-wide efforts in meeting the challenges of a transitioning energy system. The Division includes specialist areas in system planning, forecasting, future energy systems as well as onboarding and connections.

 The Market Operability team is seeking a Principal Analyst to undertake advanced energy modelling and analysis for the east coast gas and electricity systems. The team is responsible for the delivery of Gas Statement of Opportunities (GSOO), operability forecasting for the Integrated System Plan (ISP) for the National Electricity Market (NEM) and integrated gas and electricity modelling for the ISP.

 As a Principal Analyst, you will be responsible for providing strategic direction to key reports, overseeing modelling and analysis, and communicating results to various internal and external stakeholders. You will use a mix of forecast methods for the gas and electricity markets and modelling techniques focused on system optimisation over the short, medium and long-term. The Principal Analyst will lead model developments and improvements affecting short-term modelling to better analyse the reliability of the future energy system and support the development of the ISP

 Position Responsibilities:

Provide expert technical advice, expertise, and leadership on market modelling matters, including coordination of the GSOO process, leading short-term market modelling components of the ISP.

Apply advanced modelling methods, economic theory, and/or technical engineering knowledge to finalise model inputs and assumptions, run models, analyse results, and present analysis in written reports and verbal presentations.

Support the Manager in stakeholder coordination functions and facilitate industry forums to ensure that AEMO’s planning activities are effectively coordinated and communicated with stakeholders

Support the Manager in people-management related processes, such as recruitment and development of staff, training, and regular feedback and performance appraisals.

Support the Manager in stakeholder coordination functions and facilitate industry forums to ensure that AEMO’s planning activities are effectively coordinated and communicated with stakeholders

 Position Requirements

Skills & Experience:

Proven experience in energy market simulation software (preferably PLEXOS) and forecasting tools and processes.

Demonstrated experience in energy market analysis (electricity and/or gas) and the interpretation of analysis performed by others.

Demonstrable experience in managing projects and people to achieve timely delivery of specified outcomes.

Knowledge and experience in analysing the physical operation of the electricity and/or gas systems and the overarching markets, and

understanding of power system reliability assessments.

Experience in writing technical reports and other documents that convey, in simple terms, complex ideas and analysis.

Very strong communication skills, particularly the ability to present modelling and analysis, and make recommendations to internal and external stakeholders

Education/ professional Certifications: 

Tertiary and/or post graduate qualifications in mathematics, economics, data science, physics or engineering or an appropriate technology discipline.

 In return some of our benefits to you

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more.

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year.

Reward your hard work with annual performance bonuses (subject to eligibility).

Embrace a healthier you with our wellness program, discounted health insurance, gym perks and our comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP). 

 About Our Process 

At AEMO we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workforce. We welcome people of all backgrounds, including people with disabilities and neurodivergence and those who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander.

If you have any accessibility requirements to support you during the application or interview process, simply let us know by emailing us at talent@aemo.com.au

Salary: No contractor fees / two pay runs per week
Ad Text:
Business Analyst (Release and Environment Uplift)

Initial 12 month contract | Brisbane CBD | Hybrid working

Our client is embarking on a significant enterprise-wide transformation program designed to modernise its technology landscape and enhance how its stakeholders engage with the organisation. This multi-year initiative spans technology, operations, finance, risk, people, leadership and culture, representing a major investment in future-proofing core platforms while supporting strong service delivery and regulatory compliance.

We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst to join a high-profile transformation project focused on uplifting environment management, release management, and testing capability within a large enterprise setting.

This program is enabling the transition from legacy delivery models to a modern, multi-vendor SaaS ecosystem, with a strong focus on supporting scalable, high-frequency releases across multiple platforms and delivery streams.

Working closely with the Project Manager, Solution Architect, and SMEs across release, testing, and environments, you will play a key role in defining current and future state capabilities and supporting the delivery of a robust, scalable release framework.

Key Responsibilities

Elicit, analyse and document current state processes across environment, release, and testing functions

Define future state capabilities and uplift roadmap, aligned to best practice and organisational needs

Support the development of a business case within the initial project phase

Analyse interdependencies across systems, vendors, and release schedules to enable coordinated delivery

Translate high-level strategic direction into clear, actionable deliverables

Work closely with SMEs and architects to ensure alignment across technology, process, and governance layers

Contribute to pilot delivery and iterative scaling of new capabilities

Operate effectively in a fast-paced, evolving environment with ambiguous requirements

What We’re Looking For

Proven experience as a Business Analyst in complex enterprise environments

Exposure to environment management, release management, or testing/UAT coordination within multi-stream delivery programs

Experience working across end-to-end delivery lifecycles (waterfall, agile, and release phases)

Strong ability to work across multiple vendors and stakeholder groups

Solid understanding of technology architecture concepts and system interdependencies

Demonstrated ability to operate as a self-starter, with strong pragmatism and adaptability

Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Ability to work with low maturity processes and drive uplift outcomes

Why apply through Lime Recruitment?

Enjoy two pay runs per week without contractor fees. As a Queensland-owned agency, we offer personalised service and are a Tier 1 supplier to the Queensland Government. Awarded Australia’s top-performing small agency in 2021 and 2022, with a finalist spot in 2023. Partner with Lime for genuine, experienced, friendly recruiters dedicated to securing the best opportunities for you. Apply now or contact Daniel Keyte on 0433 013 200 or daniel@limejobs.com.au to find out more!

About JANA

We are here for one reason: People. Together with our clients, we can change the lives of millions of Australians for the better.
JANA is a leading investment consulting firm with offices in Melbourne & Sydney. We provide investment advisory consulting to over 85 institutional clients, including superannuation funds, charities, insurers, foundations, and endowment funds. Our services include asset allocation, manager research and selection, and investment strategy advice. We foster close relationships with each client to understand their needs and identify unique investment opportunities for their benefit.

Your new role

The focus of this role is to work closely with the Quant and Risk Function to support the wider advisory business. This opportunity is best suited to an analytically oriented and motivated candidate with strong technical skills to help develop and support the quantitative tools and models that JANA uses to undertake investment research, formulate investment advice, and provide analysis and reporting. 

This role can be based in either our Sydney or Melbourne office and reports to the Head of Quant and Risk.

Some of your responsibilities will include:

Develop, maintain, and enhance quantitative models and tools to support investment research, portfolio construction, and risk analysis
Analyse portfolio performance, attribution, and risk across multi‑asset portfolios, including equities, fixed income, cash, and derivatives.
Build and manage data pipelines and database structures using internal and external data sources (e.g. market and vendor data)
Apply statistical techniques to large and complex datasets to generate insights, reports and dashboards for internal and external stakeholders
Create and maintain programmatic models to deliver systematic and repeatable analysis
Support the development of analytics and frameworks for performance attribution, risk monitoring and portfolio evaluation
Review and improve existing Excel and code-based models across the business
Contribute to the development of capital market assumptions and asset allocation analytics
About you 

Equally challenging and rewarding, this opportunity is perfect for someone with:

Strong quantitative, analytical, and problem‑solving skills, with a high level of attention to detail
Advanced capability in Excel, Python (or similar), and SQL, with an interest in using modern tools (including AI‑assisted workflows) to improve efficiency
Experience with data visualisation or business intelligence tools (e.g. Power BI, Dash) is desirable
Comfortable working with large, complex datasets and translating analysis into clear insights
A collaborative team player who enjoys project‑based work and working with diverse stakeholders
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non‑technical audiences
A proactive, self‑motivated learner who enjoys developing new skills and improving existing systems and processes
Tertiary qualifications in a relevant discipline preferred (flexibility for the right candidate); exposure to financial markets or superannuation is advantageous but not essential
Being part of the team at JANA is just the beginning 

At JANA, we offer rewarding careers for outstanding professionals where you will receive recognition based on your performance. Further to this, JANA also provides the following benefits for its people:

50% reimbursement on costs for further study on relevant courses and study leave provisions
Flexible work options
Ability to purchase an additional 4 weeks of annual leave per year
Generous leave provisions including sick, carer’s, and parental leave options to support our people when they need it most
An annual Volunteer Day to contribute to your favourite charity or community cause
Regular health and wellbeing seminars and activities as part of our Health and Wellbeing program
Monthly reward and recognition program
Employee Assistance Program and flu vaccines
At JANA, we are committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace. JANA is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, age, or other legally protected attributes. 
   
Applications will only be considered from candidates with the appropriate working rights in Australia. Successful applicants will be required to complete a Criminal & Bankruptcy check before commencement of employment. JANA kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.
   
JANA collects your personal information in accordance with JANA’s Privacy Policy and Personal Information Collection Notice. By clicking 'Apply' or uploading documents, you consent to the collection, use and disclosure of your personal information by JANA in accordance with JANA’s Privacy Policy and Personal Information Collection Notice. This includes assessing your eligibility for the role you are applying for and keeping a copy of your CV for future vacancies if you are unsuccessful.
   
Don’t meet every single requirement but excited about this role? Studies have shown that women and people of colour are less likely to apply for jobs unless they meet all the criteria. Here at JANA, we’re passionate about creating a diverse and inclusive team—one where people can be their authentic selves. We believe diversity of thought, experience, and background strengthens our long-term excellence, ultimately ensuring prosperity for the millions of people we represent. So, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all the criteria. You just might be a great fit for another role, if not this one!

At Lite n' Easy our goal is to create a healthier Australia by changing the way Australians eat. We make it easy for people to eat well, whether they want to lose weight or simply stay healthy. As a quality driven food company, all of our creative energies go into making our meals as delicious as they are healthy.




Are you a Lean practitioner with a passion for weaving together human capability and cutting-edge technology? Lite n' Easy is looking for a specialist to spearhead our transformation journey in Brisbane.

This is newly created role is designed for a forward-thinker who can bridge the gap between today’s processes and tomorrow’s possibilities. You won’t just be managing projects; you will be the designer of our automation roadmap, partnering with senior leadership to foster a high-performance culture that thrives on innovation and steady progress.

The Role:

Reporting to the Operations Manager (QLD/NSW), you will be the driving force behind site-wide evolution:

Automation Strategy: Lead the vision for our digital future. You will oversee the integration of robotics and vision systems, guiding the journey from initial concept to the seamless commissioning of advanced technologies

Lean Leadership: Act as the site’s CI mentor. You’ll establish Lean frameworks (5S, Kanban, SMED) and facilitate Kaizen events, empowering our frontline teams to take ownership of their own efficiency and growth.

Operational Impact: Take ownership of site-wide health. Your success is measured by the tangible smoothing of workflows, the elimination of waste, and the sustained improvement of our manufacturing and supply chain agility.

Digital Insights: Translate complex operational data into clear, actionable stories. Using Power BI and advanced analytics, you will provide the roadmap that helps the business understand the long-term value of our automation investments.

About you

The Experience: A deep background in automated supply chain environments, specifically with a "boots on the ground" understanding of commissioning sophisticated picking and sorting systems.

The Methodology: A Six Sigma mindset (Green Belt or higher) with a proven ability to lead cross-functional teams through the nuances of a Lean rollout.

The Technical Edge: A natural comfort with Power BI and a working curiosity for the interplay between PLC/SCADA systems and vision technology.

The Influence: A communicator who builds bridges someone just as effective coaching on the shopfloor as they are articulating a strategic vision to the executive team.

The Foundation: A holistic understanding of how operational efficiency intersects with WHS and HACCP standards.

Why Lite n' Easy?

Shape the Future: Have a direct hand in defining what our digital and automation strategy looks like from the ground up.

Hybrid Flexibility: Enjoy a modern, Brisbane-based working model that respects your life outside of work.

High Visibility: Gain direct exposure to the Senior Leadership Team in a role where your autonomy and ideas carry significant weight.

If you're excited to take on your next challenge, lead from the front and contribute to one of Australia’s leading food companies, Apply now! Join us in shaping the future of healthy eating in Australia.  Candidates must have full work rights in Australia – Australian Citizen or New Zealand Citizen.

Ad Text:
About Us:

At Red Energy, we are passionate about providing award-winning customer service to over 1.5 million of our customers across the country. Owned by the mighty Snowy Hydro, we are 100% Australian owned which our customers love us for. Our award winning team have won 15 consecutive Canstar Blue Awards for best overall customer satisfaction amongst Victorian electricity providers. Ready for a new career - come grow with us!

The Role:

We have an exciting opportunity for a Commercial Analyst to join our Corporate Development team on an 18 month fixed term contract covering parental leave.

Reporting to the Budget and Commercial Analysis Manager, you won’t just be crunching numbers, you’ll be developing meaningful financial models that provide the roadmap for our retail business. In this role, you will be the bridge between complex data and strategic decision-making. Your core mission includes:

Support the delivery of the annual budget, Corporate Plan, and quarterly forecasts. You will be directly involved in revenue forecasting and customer-level projections to ensure our financial targets are hit.
Take a deep dive into electricity and gas gross margins, providing the business with visibility on profitability and commercial performance.
Adapting to the complex and changing energy industry to provide projections, and conduct analysis on the financial implications of changes to key business metrics against budget. 
Partner with internal stakeholders to build business cases, assess project benefits, and conduct post-implementation reviews to see how actual performance stacks up against your assumptions.
Keep us ahead of the curve by providing competitor benchmarking and industry analysis to support our market positioning.
Translate complex financial implications of non-financial data for your "internal customers," driving awareness of our commercial levers across the Retail group.
Assisting in reviewing implemented  projects to gain insights and make judgements on performance and accurate data

You will ideally have:

A degree in Finance, Accounting, or Business.
Experience in commercial advisory, business modelling, or accounting 
Advanced proficiency in Excel and SQL queries. Experience with Workday Adaptive Planning is a major plus!
Prior experience within the energy sector.
A high level of attention to detail, adaptability, and the ability to translate complex terminology into actionable insights.

The Perks: 

Competitive employee rates for your Red Energy electricity & gas bills.
Ongoing support, development and career opportunities.
Enjoy flexible working arrangements with 3 days in the office, 2 days remote.
Exceptional 30 weeks of paid parental leave for primary carers. Plus, the option to purchase 4 weeks of additional annual leave (up to 8 weeks off every year!)

You’ll be joining a diverse, high-performing team that values Safety, Agility, Decency, Courage, Ownership, and Teamwork. 

Ready to add value? Apply now to take the next step in your career!

Job Title: Senior Research Analyst, Perpetual Wealth Management
Ad Text:
More Time Off for What Matters: Enjoy up to 6 weeks of leave each year
Base yourself in our state of the art, central Sydney office
Work with our highly regarded Investments Team in this senior role


Perpetual Group is an ASX-listed company (ASX:PPT) headquartered in Sydney, Australia, currently consisting of 11 leading brands across three distinct businesses: Asset Management, Wealth Management and Corporate Trust.

About the Role
This senior role sits within the Perpetual Wealth Management Multi-Manager Investment Team and will have responsibility for researching external investment managers and contributing to the management of Perpetual Wealth Management's multi-manager, Income Alternatives programme and Fixed Income portfolio. Reporting to the Head of Income Assets, you'll research and make recommendations on a wide breadth of investment managers and opportunities, focusing on Income Alternatives and Fixed Income, and including our Responsible Investment options. You'll contribute to the design and portfolio construction of multi-manager funds, and model portfolios and the delivery of investment solutions for our clients.

You'll:

Ensure top quartile performance of our implemented (discretionary) and model portfolios, consistent with client objectives.
Design and review relevant implemented and model portfolios, focusing on the Income Alternatives and Fixed Income sectors.
Provide input into and delivery of high-quality Investment Committee and Board papers.
Contribute to strategic and dynamic asset allocation discussion and decision making and investment strategy.


About You
We're looking for someone with:

An appropriate undergraduate degree (commerce, economics, business, finance or other technical area of study e.g. science) and/or relevant investment post-graduate qualifications (e.g. CFA, CAIA, MBA, MAppFin).
5-10+ years' experience as a multi-manager research analyst and/or portfolio manager, with demonstrable experience in Income Alternatives and Fixed Income classes research.
Investment experience across traditional and alternative asset classes, including portfolio management and portfolio construction of multi-manager funds.
Strong understanding of portfolio construction theory, investment markets, and financial instruments.
Knowledge and experience with ESG factors and Responsible Investing.
Strong numeric, modelling and analytical skills, as well as strong presentation skills.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, and experience in Investment Committee reporting.
A track record of taking ownership and accountability of decisions that impact investment performance.


How we work
Our unique culture is underpinned by our three organisational behaviours, Stretch, Own it, Make an impact which drive our approach of day-to-day interactions with colleagues and clients, the way we approach decision-making to the questions we ask and the problems we solve.

Employee benefits

Work from anywhere in Australia for up to one month each year.
An annual allowance to empower you to prioritise your personal wellbeing.
Study support and commitment to supporting professional development.


Diversity and inclusion

Strong commitment to all aspects of Diversity and Inclusion through a robust 7 pillar strategy.
A growing number of employee-led networks who work to raise awareness and drive continued change.
We support over 50 First Nations Communities, helping to secure the communities' future. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander People.


We're committed to creating an inclusive workplace where diversity is celebrated. No matter your background or circumstances, we aim to provide an environment where you can thrive.

Commercial Analyst – Brisbane, QLD

Full‑time | Permanent | Global Infrastructure

We’re looking for a Commercial Analyst to help shape the future of our business by combining financial insight, strategic analysis, and sustainability perspectives

About the Role

The Commercial Analyst – Agribusiness supports commercial decision-making across Hewitt’s agribusiness portfolio through clear analysis and financial modelling. Working closely with senior leadership, you will analyse livestock, operational and market data to generate insights that inform planning, pricing and investment decisions. Your work will help drive performance, shape strategy and support the continued growth of the portfolio.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse livestock and agribusiness performance to understand key drivers across production, pricing, costs and seasonal conditions.
Develop financial models and scenario analysis to support budgets, forecasts, long-range planning and investment decisions.
Extract and interpret herd and flock performance insights from livestock management systems to inform operational improvements.
Support evaluation of acquisitions, expansion opportunities and capital projects through DCF modelling and commercial analysis.

About You

You are a commercially minded analyst with strong analytical skills and the ability to translate complex data into clear insights. By leading high-performing teams, this role champions the strategic reuse of learning assets and fosters a customer-centric culture that aligns delivery with evolving market needs.

This position requires a tech-savvy strategic thinker who can drive value-add efficiencies and innovation within the constraints of legacy systems. Beyond day-to-day management of three direct reports, the role is designed to elevate the unit’s strategic capability and scalability. The ideal leader will leverage technological advances to improve productivity and ensure deep integration across sales and operations, delivering high-quality, impactful learning experiences.

About the Role

You will be required to undertake and have experience in the following areas of expertise:

Prioritise the immediate stabilisation and optimisation of program implementation to ensure seamless transitions from contract handover to successful program launch.
Provide strategic leadership across the product portfolio and learner success, mentoring functional heads to build capability and align with long-term growth objectives.
Act as the business owner for the Navigator platform, leveraging tech advances and platform partnerships to drive functionality and modernise delivery within legacy constraints.
Champion the strategic reuse of learning assets and architectures to maximize operational efficiency, scalability, and return on investment.
Establish robust governance and consistent methodologies to ensure all programs are delivered on time, within budget, and to high-quality standards.
Foster a high-performance culture focused on innovation and value-add efficiency, representing the unit externally to strengthen market credibility and industry relationships.

About You

Tertiary qualifications or equivalent professional experience in a senior leadership capacity, ideally within a large, multi-disciplinary environment.
Proven track record in program and project management, with a sharp focus on the immediate stabilization and delivery of complex corporate or institutional programs.
Advanced people leadership skills to drive cultural change, value-add efficiency, and innovative thinking while navigating the constraints of legacy systems.
Strong business acumen and technical literacy, including the ability to act as a functional owner for learning delivery platforms and support technological scalability.
Exceptional stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and negotiate across diverse internal and external functions to ensure seamless contract-to-launch transitions.
Demonstrated expertise in governance, risk management, and process improvement to ensure all initiatives are delivered on time, on budget, and to high-quality standards.

For further information on the role & responsibilities, please refer to the Position Description

How to Apply: Submit your CV & Cover Letter detailing your interest and suitability for the job (as per the skills & experience bullet points in the job description) before Sunday 5 April by 11:55pm.

Discounts and entitlements (retail, education, fitness passport)

Job queries contact: Tai James, Talent Acquisition Consultant

E: tai.james@unsw.edu.au  (applications to the address cannot be accepted, please apply online)

UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. Applications from women, people of culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those living with disabilities, members of the LGBTIQ+ community; and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, are encouraged. Key Points

$200k-250k p.a.

Minimum NV1 Security Clearance Required

Canberra-based role

Important

If you’re available to start in July 2026 for an initial 12-month term (with potential 12-month extensions), we encourage you to apply. There is also an immediate opportunity to join the team now, with a confirmed role through June 2026 and the possibility of extension beyond.

Overview

Our client is seeking an experienced Technical Capability Analyst to support a major Defence acquisition project. The successful candidate will work closely with Project Sponsor staff to develop key capability documentation, ensuring alignment with the One Defence Capability System (ODCS), reviewing documentation generated by Delivery Agencies, and facilitating capability traceability across all project phases. This role is pivotal in supporting pre‑Gate 2 activities, shaping industry engagement, and delivering new capability into service.

Essential Requirements

Demonstrated experience in requirements / specification development and project management within Defence capability environments.

Experience working within CASG or equivalent Defence acquisition organisations.

Proven ability to develop and maintain ODCS artefacts.

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including experience supporting ATM development and pre‑Gate 2 industry engagement.

Desirable Experience

Background in Defence systems engineering.

Experience in capability management.

Experience in the conduct of tender evaluation.

Additional Information 

Our preferred candidate will be able to tailor their approach to a dynamic capability project and work in a multi-disciplinary team. They should be comfortable working in both an independent and collaborative manner, and capable of managing multiple activities concurrently. 

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. 

Standard employee screening will be conducted including an Identity check, Residential History check, Eligibility to work in Australia check, Security clearance check, Sanctions/consolidated list check and a National Police check among others. 

We look forward to reviewing your application.

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst - Lending Transformation (12-Month FTC)
Ad Text:
Company Description


Lendi Group is a market-leading digital platform business that is focused on revolutionising the entire property journey. It operates Lendi - Australia’s original and #1 digital mortgage brand - and the iconic Aussie franchise.

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Lendi Group’s loan book is more than $100b and it was named Australia’s Aggregator of the Year for 2024.

To learn more about life at Lendi Group check out our blog: https://www.lendi.com.au/inspire/category/life-at-lendi/

Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions, with a goal of becoming AI-native by June 2026.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

We are looking for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to support a large, strategic lending project focused on delivering an uplift to our home loan and lending capability. You will join the Lending team within the Experience Org and be dedicated to a major transformation and migration program, working closely with product, technology, operations and risk stakeholders to design and deliver high-quality customer, broker and team member outcomes.

This is a 12-month max-term contract, with the option to extend based on project needs and performance. 

Job Description


This is a hands-on role, focused on requirements, process design, data and operating model change in a fast-paced environment.

Lead end-to-end business analysis for complex lending initiatives, from discovery through to implementation and review.
Work across residential mortgage lending, platform and system change, core banking and servicing, including end-to-end mortgage operations.
Support transformation and migration activities, including data and process migration across platforms and providers.
Partner with technology, operations, credit, risk and compliance to deliver scalable, compliant solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Business Analysis & Requirements

Lead discovery, stakeholder workshops and define clear business requirements, user stories and acceptance criteria.
Translate complex needs into actionable artefacts for engineering, operations and vendors.

Process & Journey Design

Map current and future-state processes across origination, servicing and collections.
Identify opportunities for simplification, automation and improved customer/broker experience.
Ensure alignment with credit policy, regulatory obligations and risk controls.

Transformation & Migration

Support platform and operating model changes, including system migrations.
Contribute to data mapping, migration rules, reconciliation and cutover planning.

Delivery & Implementation

Work within cross-functional teams to prioritise backlogs and support delivery.
Support UAT, defect triage, test planning and readiness activities.
Track and communicate progress, risks and key milestones.

Risk, Compliance & Credit

Ensure alignment with regulatory requirements (e.g. NCCP, ASIC) and internal policies.
Embed credit rules, decisioning criteria and controls into processes and systems.

Stakeholder Engagement

Build strong relationships across business, technology and external partners.
Communicate complex topics clearly and support governance forums with concise insights and recommendations.

Qualifications


5+ years experience as a Business Analyst in financial services, with significant exposure to home loans / residential mortgage lending.
Demonstrated experience working on large transformation and/or migration programs, ideally involving core banking or lending platforms.
Hands-on experience with mortgage operations (e.g. settlements, servicing, collections, discharges, variations).
Experience working with or alongside risk, compliance and/or credit underwriting teams in a regulated lending environment.
Background in core banking systems, loan origination / servicing platforms or similar enterprise platforms is highly regarded.
Experience working in Agile or Hybrid delivery environments with cross-functional teams.

Nice to have

Experience in transformation programs involving third-party servicers or outsourced operations.
Exposure to APRA/ASIC regulatory change programs or risk transformation in a lending context.
Relevant qualifications in business, finance, information systems or related discipline

Additional Information


Lendi Group teams operate across Australia and the Philippines. Bringing together diverse expertise, innovative technology and a customer-first approach, our teams work seamlessly to simplify the property journey, helping Australians find, buy and own property with confidence.

We support our people in a variety of ways, but a few of the benefits that our people rave about include:

A vibrant, relaxed, yet professional culture.
Hybrid working arrangement designed to support work-life balance, while fostering meaningful connection and collaboration.
A holistic wellbeing programs offering 24/7 support, including medical, mental health, and financial wellbeing services to enable our workforce to thrive at home and work.
Generous paid Parental Leave: we celebrate our growing Lendi Group family with 18-26 weeks leave for primary carers and up to 4 weeks for secondary carers.
An additional week’s Loyalty Leave each year after reaching 3 years’ service.
Wellness initiatives with a strong focus on psychological safety.

We’re committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive community at Lendi Group. We believe that a team reflecting the world around us leads to greater innovation, stronger collaboration, and a more engaging workplace.

Our culture is guided by our 3 core values: We Are Stronger United; Act Like You Own It; and Keep Home Loans Human. Our values are part of our core DNA that helps Lendi Group to attract, engage and evolve the right talent and build best-in-class products.

This is an opportunity to shape the future of a fast-growing, purpose-driven company that’s transforming the homeownership journey.

Ready to contribute to Lendi Group’s next chapter? Apply now and be part of something big!

#lendigroup #LI-AMH1 #LI-hybrid
